chunk_summary: This section outlines Indian RDAs for protein, iron, and calcium across pediatric age groups, emphasizing practical meal planning, absorption strategies, and non-dairy calcium options for vegetarian diets.
learning_objectives: Understand Indian RDAs for protein, iron, and calcium across pediatric age groups.
Learn practical meal planning strategies using daily nutrient anchors for vegetarian diets.
Recognize the importance of dietary absorption rules and hemoglobin monitoring in iron deficiency anemia management.
key_facts: Indian RDAs for protein (g/day): 6–12 months: 11, 1–3 years: 13, 4–6 years: 18, 7–9 years: 24, 10–12 years: boys 36, girls 27.
Indian RDAs for iron (mg/day): 6–12 months: 9, 1–3 years: 9, 4–6 years: 13, 7–9 years: 16, 10–12 years: boys 21, girls 27.
Indian RDAs for calcium (mg/day): 6–12 months: 500, 1–3 years: 600, 4–6 years: 700, 7–9 years: 800, 10–12 years: 1000.
200 mL milk provides ~6–7 g protein and ~240 mg calcium; 30–40 g paneer/tofu provides ~5–8 g protein.
Non-dairy calcium sources like ragi, sesame, and fortified foods are essential for low dairy intake.
reference_quote: Use Indian RDA values as the default counseling targets, then translate them into daily protein anchors and meal structure. (ICMR-NIN 2020)
document_key_topics: Indian RDAs for protein, iron, and calcium
